name: Salt del Batlle and the pools of Santa Pau - Circular through the Natural Park of the Volcanic Zone of La Garrotxa
name_original: Salt del Batlle y las pozas de Santa Pau - Circular por el Parque Natural de la Zona Volcánica de la Garrotxa
region_intro: Hiking around Santa Pau offers access to the unique volcanic landscapes of Catalonia's Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park. The region is characterized by extinct volcanoes, ancient lava flows, and dense beech forests. Trails navigate through these geological formations, providing varied terrain and natural features for exploration. Best hiking trails around Santa Pau The most popular hiking route is Volcán de Santa Margarida and Volcán del Croscat - Circular through the Natural Park of the Volcanic Zone of La Garrotxa, a 7.6 miles (12.1 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 24 minutes to complete, exploring two prominent volcanoes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Salt del Batlle and the pools of Santa Pau - Circular through the Natural Park of the Volcanic Zone of La Garrotxa, a moderate 8.6 miles (13.8 km) path. This route features waterfalls and natural pools within the volcanic park. Local hikers also love the Puigsallanca - Circular through the Natural Park of the Volcanic Zone of La Garrotxa, an 8.6 miles (13.7 km) trail leading through the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park, often completed in about 4 hours 36 minutes. Hiking around Santa Pau is defined by volcanic craters, ancient lava flows, and lush beech forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Santa Pau are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Santa Pau's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Santa Pau?
A: Santa Pau offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 90 routes available. These trails cater to different fitness levels, including 40 easy, 46 moderate, and 5 difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable path for nearly every hiker.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for hikes in Santa Pau?
A: The hiking trails around Santa Pau range from easy to difficult. You'll find 40 easy routes, perfect for a leisurely stroll, 46 moderate trails for those seeking a bit more challenge, and 5 difficult options for experienced hikers looking for a strenuous adventure. The terrain often involves volcanic landscapes and forest paths.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Santa Pau?
A: Hiking in Santa Pau is enjoyable year-round, but autumn is particularly stunning. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4052842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fageda d'en Jordà&lt;/a&gt; beech forest transforms with vibrant golden and brown hues, offering a magical experience. Spring also brings lush greenery and pleasant temperatures, while summer can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes ideal.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes in Santa Pau?
A: Yes, many of the most popular routes around Santa Pau are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e918698190/volcan-de-santa-margarida-and-volcan-del-croscat-circular-through-the-natural-park-of-the-volcanic-zone-of-la-garrotxa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Volcán de Santa Margarida and Volcán del Croscat - Circular through the Natural Park of the Volcanic Zone of La Garrotxa&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 12.1 km loop that explores two prominent volcanoes.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see while hiking in Santa Pau?
A: Santa Pau is rich in unique natural landmarks, primarily due to its location within the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park. You can explore the craters of extinct volcanoes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1134337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Margarida Volcano&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4052842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Croscat Volcano Scoria Slopes&lt;/a&gt;, or wander through the enchanting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4052842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fageda d'en Jordà&lt;/a&gt; beech forest, which grows on ancient lava flows. Some trails also lead to beautiful spots like the Salt del Batlle waterfall.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Santa Pau?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4052842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fageda d'en Jordà&lt;/a&gt; beech forest offers easy, flat trails like the Joan Maragall Trail, which are perfect for families with children. These routes allow everyone to enjoy the unique volcanic landscape and serene forest environment.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Santa Pau?
A: The Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park is a protected area. While many trails are accessible, it's always best to check the specific regulations of the natural park regarding dogs, especially concerning leash requirements and waste disposal, to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone and protect the local wildlife.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Santa Pau?
A: The hiking experiences in Santa Pau are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 600 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the unique volcanic landscapes, the beauty of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4052842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fageda d'en Jordà&lt;/a&gt;, and the well-maintained trails that offer diverse natural features.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls or natural pools?
A: Yes, you can find routes that lead to beautiful water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937626626/salt-del-batlle-and-the-pools-of-santa-pau-circular-through-the-natural-park-of-the-volcanic-zone-of-la-garrotxa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt del Batlle and the pools of Santa Pau - Circular through the Natural Park of the Volcanic Zone of La Garrotxa&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13.8 km path that specifically highlights waterfalls and natural pools within the volcanic park.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes around Santa Pau?
A: Hike durations vary significantly depending on the route and your pace. Easy trails, like those in the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4052842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fageda d'en Jordà&lt;/a&gt;, can be as short as an hour. Moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e918698190/volcan-de-santa-margarida-and-volcan-del-croscat-circular-through-the-natural-park-of-the-volcanic-zone-of-la-garrotxa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Volcán de Santa Margarida and Volcán del Croscat circular&lt;/a&gt;, typically take around 3-4 hours, while more challenging trails can extend to 5 hours or more.

Q: Are there viewpoints offering panoramic views of the region?
A: Yes, several trails in the Santa Pau area lead to viewpoints that offer stunning panoramic views of the volcanic landscape, valleys, and surrounding natural park. These include vantage points from areas like Sant Julià del Mont and Portal del Mar, providing excellent photo opportunities.

Q: Is there parking available for hikers near the trails?
A: Yes, parking facilities are generally available near popular trailheads and visitor centers within the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park. It's advisable to check specific parking locations for your chosen route, especially during peak seasons, to ensure convenience.
tips: This beautiful and quiet waterfall is located shortly after passing the urban center of the town of Santa Pau towards Mieres, within the river basin of the Sant Martí stream before flowing into the Ser river.

The Santuario dels Arcs is an architectural complex in Santa Pau (Garrotxa) that forms part of the Inventory of the Architectural Heritage of Catalonia.
There are four buildings organized around a covered patio that can be accessed through semicircular arched porticoes. The voussoired door with the date of 1567 stands out.

Santa Pau is a municipality from Girona located in the region of La Garrotxa, Catalonia.

It is located in the very center of the natural park of the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone, between the municipalities of Olot and Mieras. It is surrounded by several volcanic cones such as Croscat, Roca Negra and Santa Margarita and by the Finestres, Corb and Sant Juliá del Mont mountain ranges.

waypoints.name: Can Cotilla Waterfall (or Molí de Can Riera Waterfall)
